title: toxicoscordion micranthum
intro: Toxicoscordion micranthum, also known as poison camas, is a perennial plant native to the western United States and Canada, characterized by its white or purple flowers and narrow leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antispasmodic effects. The health benefits of Toxicoscordion micranthum include relieving respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as reducing swelling and pain. Toxicoscordion micranthum can be prepared as a tincture, tea, or salve, and is sometimes used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
